seo.title: Seedance 1.5 Pro | AI Video Model, Specs & Pricing | MaxVideoAI
seo.description: Use the supported Seedance 1.5 Pro model on MaxVideoAI for short cinematic clips, then move to Seedance 2.0 for the current Seedance workflow.

overview: Seedance 1.5 Pro remains available as a supported older Seedance Pro workflow for short cinematic clips, repeatable variants, and camera-fixed setups.
pricingNotes: Pricing varies by resolution; audio off costs about 50% less than audio on.

hero.title: Seedance 1.5 Pro — supported older Seedance model for short cinematic clips
hero.intro: Seedance 1.5 Pro remains available as a supported older Seedance Pro workflow for short, repeatable cinematic clips, while Seedance 2.0 is now the main Seedance model for stronger multi-shot continuity, richer audio, and broader reference workflows.

items.title: Product shots with controlled motion (clean, repeatable takes)
Smooth continuity with end frames (I2V resolves / match landings)
Repeatable looks with seed control (variant testing)
Short ads and social clips with audio on/off
Moodboards / key beats before longer edits

technicalOverview.label: Details
technicalOverview.body: Seedance 1.5 Pro is a short-form audio-video model with strong control knobs: camera_fixed for locked-off shots, seed for repeatable iterations, and optional end frame for smoother I2V landings. Use it when you want steadier motion, consistent variants, and audio-ready drafts in 4–12 second clips.

technicalOverview.label: Inputs
technicalOverview.body: Text → Video; Image → Video with optional end frame (PNG/JPG/WebP)

technicalOverview.label: Controls
technicalOverview.body: Seed (-1 for random), camera fixed, safety checker

technicalOverview.label: Audio
technicalOverview.body: On by default; toggle off for lower cost

promptStructure.title: Prompting Seedance 1.5 Pro
promptStructure.quote: [Subject] [one clear action] in [setting], camera_fixed, [lighting/style], [duration], optional audio.
promptStructure.description: Keep prompts shot-level, favor camera_fixed for steadier motion, and add concise audio notes if enabled.

tips.title: Tips & limits
items: Use camera_fixed for locked-off shots and steadier motion.
Add an end frame for smoother I2V resolves.
Reuse a seed for repeatable variations.
Keep clips short (4–12s) for best coherence.

prompts.title: Locked camera neon alley
prompts.prompt: A moody 16:9 neon alley in light rain, camera locked, reflective puddles, subtle ambience, soft audio bed.

prompts.title: Image to video with end frame
prompts.prompt: Animate the start image with a slow push-in, then resolve into the provided end frame, gentle ambience.

faqs.a: It locks the camera for steadier motion and stronger continuity between frames.
Yes. Audio is on by default but you can toggle it off for lower cost.

faqs.q: How should I use seed control?
faqs.a: Use -1 for random exploration, then reuse a fixed seed when you want to compare small prompt, camera_fixed, timing or audio changes without changing the whole look.

faqs.q: Does Seedance 1.5 Pro support start and end frames?
faqs.a: Yes for image-to-video: use one start image and an optional end frame when the final pose, product position or transition landing matters.
T2V supports 480p and 720p. I2V supports 480p, 720p, and 1080p.

faqs.q: Is Seedance 1.5 Pro still the main Seedance Pro workflow on MaxVideoAI?
faqs.a: Seedance 1.5 Pro is the supported older Seedance Pro workflow on MaxVideoAI, but Seedance 2.0 is now the main current Seedance model for broader production work.

heroHighlights: Camera-fixed stability||Lock the camera for steadier motion and cleaner results in city/product scenes
Seed control for repeatability||Reuse a seed to iterate without the look drifting — great for campaign variants and A/B hooks
Start + end frame control (I2V)||Provide an optional end frame to guide how the shot lands (transitions, match cuts, clean finishes)
Native audio in the same render||Generate dialogue, ambience, and SFX in one pass — or toggle audio off for silent drafts and post finishing
custom.specTitle: Real Specs – Seedance 1.5 Pro in MaxVideoAI
custom.specNote: Specs reflect the live Fal routing today.

specSections.title: Camera-fixed stability
specSections.intro: camera_fixed locks the shot for steadier motion in short-form clips.

specSections.title: Repeatability + control
specSections.intro: Seed control and end frames help you iterate and land transitions cleanly.
items: Optional end frame for I2V landings.
Audio on/off in the same render.

strengths: Use camera_fixed for locked-off shots and steadier motion.
Reuse a seed for repeatable variations.
Add an end frame for smoother I2V resolves and cleaner landings.
Keep clips short (4–12s) and actions simple for best coherence.
Use audio sparingly: one short line + ambience + 1 key SFX.
troubleshootingItems: Motion feels messy → enable camera_fixed; reduce action speed; simplify background.
Drift across takes → lock a seed; keep the same prompt structure; change one variable.
Audio/lip sync drifts → shorten lines; avoid fast head turns; keep the shot calmer.
Random text/logos → strengthen negative ("no text, no logos, no UI") and keep signage out of frame.
boundaries: Resolution and pricing vary by tier; check the live spec chips before rendering.
Still no reliable tiny on-screen text — overlay critical copy in post.

hero.title: Seedance 1.5 Pro
hero.subtitle: Camera-fixed shots, seeded variants and start/end frame control for supported older Seedance workflows.
hero.paragraph: Use Seedance 1.5 Pro when you need the older supported Seedance route: repeatable short clips, optional audio, seed control, camera-fixed setups and image-to-video landings. Use Seedance 2.0 for current 2.0 production work.

features.title: Camera fixed
features.body: Lock the shot when steadier motion matters more than dynamic camera moves.

features.title: Seed control
features.body: Reuse a seed for repeatable variants and A/B creative passes.

features.title: Start/end frames
features.body: Guide image-to-video landings with an optional final frame.

features.title: Audio on/off
features.body: Generate with native sound, or switch audio off for lower-cost silent drafts.

features.title: 1080p on I2V
features.body: Use 1080p on image-to-video checks; text-to-video stays on 480p or 720p presets.

features.title: Pay-as-you-go
features.body: See exact live price before you generate.

decisionCards.title: Use 1.5 Pro or Seedance 2.0?
decisionCards.body: Use 1.5 Pro for legacy-compatible camera-fixed and seeded tests. Use Seedance 2.0 for the current native-audio production workflow.

decisionCards.title: Need repeatable variants?
decisionCards.body: Keep one prompt structure, reuse seed control, then change only the action, timing or audio cue you are testing.

decisionCards.title: Planning final Seedance work?
decisionCards.body: Move current campaign finals to Seedance 2.0 when multi-shot continuity and broader references matter more.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text prompt
referenceWorkflows.body: Write one subject, one action, lighting, style and an optional audio cue.

referenceWorkflows.title: Camera-fixed setup
referenceWorkflows.body: Use camera_fixed for locked-off product, city or storyboard shots.

referenceWorkflows.title: Image-to-video
referenceWorkflows.body: Start from one image, then add an end frame when the landing pose matters.

referenceWorkflows.title: Seeded variant
referenceWorkflows.body: Reuse the same seed to compare small prompt changes without changing the whole look.

referenceWorkflows.title: Upgrade path
referenceWorkflows.body: Use Seedance 2.0 when the project needs the current 2.0 workflow.

section.title: How to Write a Great Seedance 1.5 Pro Prompt
section.intro: Seedance 1.5 Pro performs best with clear subject/action, controlled camera direction (often camera-fixed), and concise audio notes when audio is enabled.

tabs.label: Quick
tabs.title: Quick prompt (fast iteration)
tabs.description: Use 1–2 sentences when you want variations.
tabs.copy:
[Subject] [one clear action] in [setting], [framing], [lighting/style].
Audio (optional): [ambience + 1 key SFX OR one short line].
Negative: no text, no logos, no subtitles/overlays.

tabs.label: Structured
tabs.title: Structured prompt (best for reliable results)
tabs.description: Separate information so the model can follow it consistently.
tabs.copy:
Subject:
[Who/what + 2–3 defining details.]

Setting:
[Where + time + 2 anchors.]

Camera:
[Framing + (camera_fixed recommended) OR one simple move.]

Look:
[Lighting + palette anchors.]

Audio (optional):
Dialogue: “Short line.”
Ambience + 1–2 SFX cues.

Negative:
[Text, logos, extra people, blur.]

tabs.label: Pro
tabs.title: Pro prompt (ultra-specific "film crew brief")
tabs.description: Use this when you need a very specific cinematic look or continuity across shots.
tabs.copy:
Director brief:
[Goal + vibe.]

Continuity anchors:
- Subject identity + wardrobe/props
- Location + time + 3 visual anchors
- Lighting + palette anchors

Camera:
- Framing + angle
- Prefer camera_fixed for steadiness (or one simple move)

Audio (if on):
- Dialogue: “One short line.”
- Ambience + 1–2 SFX cues (keep it minimal)

Seed (recommended for iterations):
Reuse the same seed when generating variants.

tabs.label: Storyboard
tabs.title: Storyboard prompt (sequenced beats)
tabs.description: Use this when you want a mini-story in one clip. A storyboard prompt (aka sequenced beats) gives clear timing, camera direction, and continuity.
tabs.copy:
Duration: [4–12s] • Aspect: [21:9 / 16:9 / 4:3 / 1:1 / 3:4 / 9:16] • Audio: [on/off]

Beat 1 (0–4s):
[Framing + action + camera direction]

Beat 2 (4–8s):
[Framing + action + camera direction]

Beat 3 (8–12s):
[Final action/reveal + settle]
End frame (optional, I2V): [describe the final frame you want to land on]

Audio (if on):
Dialogue: “Short line.”
Ambience + 1–2 SFX cues.

Constraints:
No logos. No readable text. No subtitles/overlays.

tabNotes.body: Quick = variations. Use for fast iteration.
Structured = consistency. Use when you need reliable results.
Pro = control. Use for precise, repeatable looks.
Storyboard = beat timing. Use for short sequenced clips.

globalPrinciples: Prefer camera-fixed shots when you want steadier motion and fewer artifacts.
Keep one readable action per clip (or per beat).
Use a seed when iterating — change one variable at a time.
If audio is on: keep dialogue short and add only 1–2 sound cues.
engineWhy: camera_fixed is a strength: use it for locked camera realism and product shots.
Seedance is short-form: keep beats simple and avoid cramming multiple actions.
End frame (I2V) helps land transitions; don’t introduce new actions in the last second.
Audio is best with minimal cues; long monologues can drift.
If text matters, plan to overlay it in post (tiny text is unreliable).

demo.title: Demo prompt — Seedance 1.5 Pro
demo.prompt:
10s Seedance 1.5 Pro clip in 16:9, audio on.
A motorcycle rider crosses a wet street at night and slows inside the frame.
camera_fixed enabled: low locked shot, slight push-in, one readable motion beat.
Night cinematic look, wet street reflections, simple background, no added text.
Audio: short engine cue, light rain, street ambience, no dialogue.
Seed: reuse the same seed when comparing only lighting, timing or audio intensity.

summary.subject: Motorcycle rider on a wet street
summary.action: Motorcycle approaches and slows inside the frame
summary.style: Night cinematic look, wet street reflections
summary.output: Short engine cue, light rain, no dialogue
